steps.title: Recognize the logical gap in “entirely attributable”
steps.content: The student claims the reduction is “entirely” due to lizards. To weaken an exclusive causal claim, you need evidence the same outcome also occurs without that cause.

steps.title: Identify the weakening data
steps.content: The graph shows the no-lizards enclosure also declined substantially (from ~85 to ~42 by day 30), proving factors other than lizards drive population loss, which contradicts the “entirely attributable to lizards” claim. Choice A is just baseline data, C is timing information unrelated to causation, and D actually supports the student's conclusion. That matches choice B — B is correct.
